tools.name: smart_compact
tools.description: [compact] Use when: need to analyze tool history and identify which outputs can be safely dropped or summarized to free context budget. Rules-based (zero LLM cost). Returns droppable indices, summarizable entries, recovery context, and estimated token savings.

toolHistory.description: Array of tool call entries: [{ tool, ok, result?, error?, timestamp? }]. Auto-populated from server context when auto:true.

auto.description: Auto mode: server injects toolHistory from current session context. No need to pass toolHistory manually.

currentGoal.description: Current task goal for recovery context

currentTodos.description: Current TODO items for recovery context

tools.name: smart_read
tools.description:
Progressive file reader — FULLY replaces raw read (text + dirs + images).
  Session cache: same file unchanged = zero disk reads.

  🥇 auto      — Smart mode by file size (<50 full, 50-300 sig, >300 outline)
  📋 outline   — Structure: function/class/variable declarations + line numbers
  📝 signatures — Structure + signature text + line ranges
  🔍 symbol    — Extract specific symbol full body (by name)
  🧠 explain   — Symbol + imports + callers in one call
  📏 range     — Specific line range (startLine/endLine) + checksum
  📄 full      — Traditional full read, offset/limit paging
  📚 batch     — Read multiple files in one call
  🗺  project   — Project symbol map <500 tokens
  🖼  image     — PNG/JPG/GIF/WebP returned as viewable attachment
  📁 directory  — Auto-detected, returns sorted listing

  Output: text (default), compact, json
  Session cache: repeat reads = zero disk I/O

file.description: File path (relative to root or absolute)

limit.description: Max lines to read (for full/range mode)

endLine.description: End line (1-indexed, inclusive, for range mode)

numbered.description: Include line numbers (full/range mode, default: true)

tools.name: smart_rules
tools.description: Use when: need to check project rules (AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, etc.) before editing. Discovers and reads project-level rule files. Use before any edit to ensure compliance with project conventions.

file.description: Target file path — returns rules applicable to this file

inject.description: Inject matching rules as context (default: true)

tools.name: smart_think
tools.description:
Conversational reasoning engine — surpasses sequential-thinking.

Default mode: mode:"cit" (BN-DP auto-branch). Only branches when uncertain — saves ~70% tokens on routine tasks.
  mode:"beam" → explore 2-3 alternative paths with confidence scoring. Use for high-risk decisions.
  mode:"forest" → multi-tree reasoning with consensus voting. Use for complex multi-angle problems.

Core workflow:
  thought + nextThoughtNeeded=true → hypothesis → verification → repeat → nextThoughtNeeded=false

Supports: template guidance (debug/refactor/architecture...), branchFromThought, isRevision,
needsMoreThoughts (beyond initial plan), adjustTotalThoughts (mid-stream expansion).
Returns done flag + optional updated totalThoughts.

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether more reasoning steps are needed

mode.description: Reasoning mode (default: "cit" — BN-DP auto-branch, only branches when uncertain). "beam" for high-risk multi-path exploration. "forest" for multi-angle consensus voting. "structured" for Grammar-Constrained CoT (GOAL/STATE/ALGO/EDGE/VERIFY — saves 50-70% thinking tokens).

beams.description: Pre-defined beam paths for multi-path reasoning. Each beam: {name, content, confidence}. Used with mode:"beam" or mode:"cit" when branchingNeeded=true.

content.description: The reasoning content for this path

selectedBeam.description: Name of the selected/correct beam path (e.g. "Path C"). Used with mode:"beam" or mode:"cit" when branchingNeeded=true.

branchingNeeded.description: CiT BN-DP assessment result. true = branching needed (explore multiple paths). false = chain mode (single path sufficient). Used with mode:"cit" only.

branchReasoning.description: CiT BN-DP reasoning: why branching is or is not needed at this step. Used with mode:"cit" only.

trees.description: Forest-of-Thought trees. Each: {name:string, branches:[{name,content,confidence}], selectedBranch:string}. Used with mode:"forest".

selectedBranch.description: Name of the best branch in this tree

consensus.description: Forest-of-Thought consensus result — cross-tree voting. Used with mode:"forest".

conclusion.description: Winning conclusion from across all trees

agreeingTrees.description: Tree names that agree on the conclusion

hypothesis.description: Generate a testable hypothesis. Triggers structured hypothesis output section.

verification.description: Verify a previous hypothesis. Auto-detects pass/fail from content (confirm/reject). Triggers structured verification output section with verdict icon.

needsMoreThoughts.description: Signal that more reasoning steps are needed beyond initial totalThoughts plan. Shows adjusted total in output.

adjustTotalThoughts.description: Adjust totalThoughts upward mid-stream (e.g. when new sub-problems discovered). Overrides totalThoughts for display.

revisesThought.description: Which thought number is being revised

template.description: Optional template for structured reasoning guidance

goal.description: GOAL: One-sentence objective. Used with mode:"structured".

state.description: STATE: Known information and context. Used with mode:"structured".

algo.description: ALGO: Reasoning path and method. Used with mode:"structured".

edge.description: EDGE: Boundary conditions and constraints. Used with mode:"structured".

verify.description: VERIFY: Self-verification logic. Used with mode:"structured".

tools.name: smart_exa_crawl
tools.description:
Crawl web pages and extract their content. Auto-detects static vs JS sites (no need to specify renderer).

Use with options for better results:
  - clean: (recommended) For news, blogs, articles — removes nav/ads/footer, saves 40-60% tokens
  - markdown: For LLM-friendly output — converts to Markdown, preserves structure
  - chunk: For long articles with 5+ sections — splits by heading, saves LLM context
  - crawlee: (advanced) For complex sites — auto-detects static vs JS, retries on failure
  - render: For JS-heavy sites with no visible content — uses Playwright rendering

Do NOT use for: searching the web (use smart_exa_search). Operating a browser (use smart_pw_browser).

clean.description: Article extraction: removes nav/ads/footer. Use for news, blogs, tutorials. (recommended)

markdown.description: Convert to Markdown. Use when the result feeds into an LLM. Saves ~60% tokens vs raw HTML.

chunk.description: Split long content by heading. Use when article has 5+ sections. Saves LLM context window.

maxChunkSize.description: Max chars per chunk when --chunk is used (default: 2000)

crawlee.description: Adaptive crawl: auto-detects static vs JS-heavy sites. Use for complex sites that might need JS rendering. Requires npm install crawlee.

render.description: Render JS-heavy pages with Playwright. Use when content is empty/missing without JS.

extended.description: Extended mode — up to 30,000 chars per result. Use for very long documents.

compress.description: Compress output to save tokens. "caveman" strips grammar, keeps facts (15-30% token savings). Default: none.

compressLevel.description: Caveman compression level. light=stop-words only, semantic=content selection, aggressive=full lemmatization, ultra=abbreviations+arrows (50-70% savings). Default: semantic.

tools.name: smart_exa_search
tools.description:
Search the web or code/documentation with natural language queries. Returns structured results with URLs and summaries.

Use for:
  - Web search: finding recent articles, docs, news (command: "search")
  - Code search: finding code examples, API usage patterns (command: "code")

Do NOT use for: crawling a specific URL (use smart_exa_crawl). Operating a browser (use smart_pw_browser).

command.description: search: web search for information. code: find code examples and docs.

compress.description: Compress output to save tokens. "caveman" strips grammar, keeps facts (15-30% token savings). Default: none.

compressLevel.description: Caveman compression level. light=stop-words only, semantic=content selection, aggressive=full lemmatization, ultra=abbreviations+arrows (50-70% savings). Default: semantic.

tools.name: smart_fast_apply
tools.description:
Unified editing tool — replaces write + edit + edit_ast.
Supports 7 input formats (ordered by token efficiency):
  - block-diff: symbol-aware block editing (NEW, most reliable). Specify {file, symbol, newContent, action?}. No fuzzy matching needed.
  - unified-diff: git diff format — MOST token-efficient (40-60% savings). Use +/- lines only, no unchanged lines needed.
  - lazy: SEARCH/REPLACE with // ... existing code ... markers (80-98% savings for large files)
  - hashline: line-number + content-hash addressing — MOST ROBUST for large files (>400 lines). Specify line range directly. No fuzzy match ambiguity.
  - partial: abbreviated SEARCH context (fewer lines, L5 fuzzy matching)
  - search-replace: standard SEARCH/REPLACE blocks (Aider-compatible)
  - whole-file: full file replacement (most tokens)
💡 Tip: block-diff for symbol-level edits, unified-diff for small edits, hashline for >400 line files, lazy for large files with few changes.
Features: 6-level fuzzy matching (L6 = gap-tolerant subsequence), hashline addressing with content verification, atomic multi-file apply, undo snapshots, binary/access checks.
Dry-run by default — safe to use without side effects.

qualityGates.message: Security fix requires multi-path analysis first. Run smart_think({mode:"beam"}) before applying fixes.

format.description: Input format (default: search-replace). Token efficiency: unified-diff (best, +/- only) > lazy > hashline > partial > search-replace > whole-file. Use hashline for large files (>400 lines) where SEARCH/REPLACE matching is unreliable. sed: single sed expression. multi-hunk: multiple sed/search-replace in one file. batch: glob+sed across multiple files.

search.description: Text to search (multi-line). For lazy format: use // ... markers to skip unchanged code. For partial format: abbreviated context lines.

newContent.description: Replacement content for block-diff format (paired with symbol)

blocks.description: Edit blocks. For search-replace/lazy/partial: {file,search,replace}. For block-diff: {file,symbol,newContent,action?}.

text.description: Raw text containing SEARCH/REPLACE blocks or unified diff (auto-parsed)

diff.description: Unified diff string (format=unified-diff). Token-efficient: only +/- lines needed, omit unchanged context.

oldContent.description: Expected content of the line range (for verification)

changes.description: Hashline edit changes (for format=hashline). Specify line range directly with content verification — most robust for large files.

sed.description: Sed expression (required for format=sed or format=batch). E.g. "s/foo/bar/g", "/pattern/d".

search.description: Text to search within hunk range

hunks.description: Hunks array for format=multi-hunk. Each hunk can have sed or search+replace, optionally restricted to line range.

line.description: Line number (1-indexed). For format=sed: restrict sed to this line (or line+endLine range).

endLine.description: End line (inclusive, 1-indexed). Pair with line for sed range restriction.

file.description: Target file path (flat syntax). Combine with content to create file, or with search+replace to edit.

search.description: Text to find (flat syntax, paired with replace and file)

replace.description: Replacement text (flat syntax, paired with search and file)

content.description: New file content (flat syntax, paired with file — creates or overwrites entire file)

symbol.description: Symbol name (function/class) for symbol-aware editing. Combines with action+newContent to edit a specific symbol body.

newContent.description: Content for symbol-aware editing (paired with symbol+action) or block-boundary action (paired with action+startLine)

action.description: Edit action. With symbol: append/prepend/replace. With startLine: insert-before/insert-after/delete.

files.description: Restrict changes to these files only

validate.description: Check brace/bracket balance after apply (default: false)

atomic.description: Apply all changes atomically with rollback on failure (default: false)

dryRun.description: Preview only — show changes without applying. Without this flag or apply:true, safe single-file edits auto-apply immediately.

apply.description: Actually apply changes (required for 3+ files). Without this flag or dryRun:true, safe single-file edits auto-apply immediately.

output.description: Output format (default: text). Use "ansi" for terminal-colored diff output (works in iTerm2 headless mode).

tools.name: smart_github_search
tools.description: Use when: need to find real-world code examples from public GitHub repos. Filter by repo, path, language, case. Best for: seeing how others use an API, finding production patterns. Avoid when: need to search your own codebase (use grep instead).

tools.name: smart_glob
tools.description: Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size. Supports glob patterns like "**/*.js" or "src/**/*.ts". Returns matching file paths. Use when: need to find files by name patterns. Avoid when: doing open-ended search requiring multiple rounds of globbing and grepping (use Task tool instead).

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match files against

path.description: The directory to search in. If not specified, the current working directory will be used. IMPORTANT: Omit this field to use the default directory. DO NOT enter "undefined" or "null" - simply omit it for the default behavior. Must be a valid directory path if provided.

tools.name: smart_grep
tools.description: Use when: need to search codebase by regex. Returns function/class scope context + import graph for matched code. Avoid when: matching exact filename (use glob instead).

invert.description: Invert match — show lines that do NOT match (like grep -v)

countOnly.description: Only show match counts per file (like grep -c)

fileTypes.description: File type filter: "all" for any file, or comma-separated extensions like ".txt,.log,.md"

semantic.description: Enable hybrid semantic search (BM25 + TF-IDF fusion)

semanticWeight.description: Custom semantic weight 0.0-1.0 (auto-detected from query type)

tools.name: smart_arch_overview
tools.description: Use when: need to understand project architecture in one shot. Returns structured JSON with layers, dependencies, violations, critical functions, and unused exports. LLM should call this first when entering a new codebase.

build.description: Force CKG build if not built yet (default: true)

tools.name: smart_learn
tools.description: Use when: need to understand project structure, tech stack, deps, coding style. Runs full analysis pipeline including AST parsing. Start here when entering a new codebase. Avoid when: just searching for code (use grep instead).

tools.name: smart_lsp
tools.description: [lsp] Use when: need type-aware code understanding — find definitions, references, hover types, symbols, or diagnostics. Auto-detects language from file extension. Supports TypeScript/JS, Python, Rust, Swift, PHP. Avoid when: searching by text pattern (use smart_grep instead).

operation.description: LSP operation: symbols (list all), references (find usages), hover (type info), definition (jump to def), diagnostics (errors/warnings)

file.description: File path relative to project root (e.g. "src/auth.ts")

line.description: Line number (1-indexed). Required for references, hover, definition.

character.description: Character offset (0-indexed). Required for references, hover, definition.

root.description: Project root directory (default: current working directory)

tools.name: smart_security
tools.description: Use when: need to scan project for credentials leaks, injection flaws, path traversal, or dependency vulnerabilities. Run before committing or before CI. Avoid when: looking for general code quality issues (use lint instead).

failOn.description: Fail if finding at severity >= this

tools.name: smart_test
tools.description: Use when: need to discover or run project tests. Auto-detects vitest/jest/mocha/ava/node:test. Supports watch mode for iterative development. Avoid when: checking test coverage (use coverage instead).

include.description: Test file glob pattern to include

tools.name: smart_deep_think
tools.description: Use when: need structured reasoning — analyzing complex problems, debugging, making decisions, researching, or planning. Supports 10 templates (debug/refactor/feature/research/decision/analyze/plan_execute/retrospect/architecture/peer_review) + 3 modes (static/iterative/dynamic). Avoid when: need quick answer to simple question (use quick-think instead).

topic.description: Topic/question to analyze (required for static/dynamic modes)

format.description: Output format for static mode (default: text)

plan.description: JSON plan string (from planner) for plan_execute context

iterative.description: Interactive mode: one step at a time with prompts

dynamic.description: Start dynamic reasoning session (state-file based multi-round). Use with topic + optional template.

state.description: State file path for dynamic mode (default: ~/.smart/thinking-state.json)

record.description: Record result for step: "idx:result" format, e.g. "2:The error is TypeError"

advance.description: Advance to next thinking step after recording result

branch.description: Select branch path by name after completing a step (e.g. "hypothesis-confirmed", "needs-deeper")

finish.description: Mark thinking session complete, show full summary

status.description: Show current thinking state without advancing

restore.description: Load and display saved state file (shorthand for status)

tools.name: smart_academic_review
tools.description:
Academic peer review using the Remi 10-point framework (Nature/Science level).

Evaluates manuscripts across 10 dimensions:
  1. Scientific quality and novelty
  2. Methodology and assumptions
  3. Consistency and coherence
  4. Results and interpretation
  5. Figures, tables, and presentation
  6. Literature review
  7. Impact and relevance
  8. Meta-commentary & Tone (AI fluff detection)
  9. Major and minor issues
  10. Final recommendation (Accept/Minor/Major/Reject)

Also checks for banned AI vocabulary: delve, tapestry, crucial, testament, realm, fosters, underscores, moreover.

Modes:
  - mode:"prompt" → Returns the review prompt for LLM to execute (default)
  - mode:"template" → Returns a structured fill-in template
  - mode:"framework" → Returns just the 10-point framework definition

Examples:
  { text: "Full manuscript text...", mode: "prompt" }
  { text: "Methods section...", section: "methods", mode: "template" }

mode.description: Output mode: prompt (review prompt for LLM), template (structured fill-in), framework (definition only). Default: prompt.

section.description: Focus review on a specific section (e.g., "methods", "results", "introduction")

tools.name: smart_academic_search
tools.description:
Search academic literature across free databases: OpenAlex, Crossref, Semantic Scholar, and Unpaywall.

Sources:
  - openalex: Full-text search with abstracts, MDPI filtering, OA detection
  - crossref: Broad metadata search with DOI resolution
  - semantic_scholar: AI-powered search with citation counts and OA PDF links
  - unpaywall: Check if a specific DOI has an Open Access version available

Operations:
  - source:"openalex" → Search papers by keyword (supports year range, MDPI filter)
  - source:"crossref" → Search papers by keyword or resolve a single DOI
  - source:"semantic_scholar" → AI-powered paper search with citation data
  - source:"unpaywall" → Check OA availability for a specific DOI

Examples:
  { query: "urban heat island vegetation", source: "openalex", yearFrom: 2020 }
  { doi: "10.1038/ncomms14196", source: "crossref" }
  { doi: "10.1038/ncomms14196", source: "unpaywall", email: "user@example.com" }
  { query: "climate policy acceptance", source: "semantic_scholar", limit: 5 }

query.description: Search query string (required for openalex, crossref, semantic_scholar)

doi.description: DOI to resolve or check OA status (for crossref single lookup or unpaywall)

yearTo.description: Filter papers up to this year (inclusive)

filterMDPI.description: Filter out MDPI publications (default: true for OpenAlex)

tools.name: smart_adr
tools.description: Record, search, and list Architecture Decision Records. Stores why decisions were made (not just what). Complements KG memory.

command.description: Command: record (create), search (find by keyword), list (all ADRs), get (by ID), update (change status), delete

context.description: Background and context for the decision

decision.description: The decision that was made (required for record)

tools.name: smart_agent_execute
tools.description: Use when: the task requires 5+ tools and you want a complete workflow automation plan. Especially useful for small/weak models. Generates the full workflow lifecycle: template selection → create command → dispatch → replan → summary. Avoid when: the task is simple (1-2 tools).

goal.description: Task description for workflow automation (e.g. "debug login error", "refactor user authentication")

tools.name: smart_agent_plan
tools.description: Use when: the goal is complex, ambiguous, or requires 5+ steps — delegate decomposition to the planner engine. Especially useful for small/weak models. Returns a structured plan with DAG dependencies and parallel hints. Avoid when: the goal is simple and already clear.

goal.description: Complex task description to decompose (e.g. "find and fix all security vulnerabilities")

steps.description: Maximum steps in the plan (default: auto)

strict.description: Strict mode — only use explicitly matching templates (default: false)

state.description: State file path for tracking plan execution

tools.name: smart_agent_recommend
tools.description: Use when: the LLM is unsure which smart-mcp tool to use for a given goal, or wants a deterministic tool recommendation. Analyzes the goal and returns the optimal tool chain. Now powered by the same unified classifier as hybrid_router. Also useful for small/weak models that benefit from code-driven tool selection. Avoid when: you already know which tool to use.

goal.description: Task description to analyze (e.g. "debug login error", "refactor auth module")

context.description: Optional context about what has been done already (e.g. "already used smart_grep") — appended to goal for richer matching

tools.name: smart_autofix
tools.description: Apply a code fix and automatically verify with test/lint/security. Returns pass/fail with details so LLM can retry if needed.

fix.description: The fix to apply — unified diff or SEARCH/REPLACE block

verify.description: Verification steps to run after applying the fix (default: ["test"])

files.description: Files affected by this fix (for targeted verification)

root.description: Project root directory (default: current working directory)

tools.name: smart_code_ast
tools.description:
Query code structure via AST. Use when: need to find function/class/interface/type/variable definitions, inspect file structure, or understand symbol locations WITHOUT guessing.

Supports filtering by symbol name and kind (function, class, interface, type, variable). Output includes line numbers, column positions, and recursive children.

Phase 10: LSP-based code intelligence tool.

file.description: Target file path (relative to project root)

tools.name: smart_code_call_graph
tools.description:
Trace function call relationships across your codebase. Use when: need to understand who calls a function (callers) or what a function calls (callees), before refactoring or debugging.

Supports configurable depth (1-3 levels) and cross-file tracking. Cannot determine callees precisely without AST parsing (Phase 10/Tree-sitter upgrade planned).

direction.description: Trace direction: callers (who calls it) or callees (what it calls). Default: callers

tools.name: smart_code_impact
tools.description:
Analyze impact radius of code changes. Use when: planning a refactor, need to know what depends on a function/module, or want to assess risk before making changes.

Supports both git-diff input and direct file+symbol queries. Uses LSP references + call-graph to trace transitive impact. Conservative over-approximation for dynamic languages.

files.description: Array of file paths to analyze impact for (alternative to diff)

symbols.description: Symbol names to trace (default: all exported symbols in files)

diff.description: Git diff text to parse for changes

tools.name: smart_code_query
tools.description:
Query Code Knowledge Graph (CKG) — persistent project-wide code analysis.

Queries:
  build            — Full project scan (creates CKG database)
  update           — Incremental update for a single file
  callers          — Who calls a symbol (with depth control)
  callees          — What a symbol calls
  dependencies     — Import structure of a file
  unused-exports   — Find exported symbols not imported by other files
  symbol           — Look up symbol definitions
  stats            — CKG statistics

Phase 11: Builds on LSP bridge to create a persistent SQLite-based knowledge graph.
CKG is stored at ~/.smart/ckg/ and persists across sessions.

Phase C.2: Test Coverage Map — CKG records which tests cover which functions.
  - test-coverage         — Query which tests cover a specific source function
  - test-coverage-file    — Query coverage map for an entire source file
  - build-coverage        — Build/rebuild test coverage map (call after build)

Examples:
  { query: "build", root: "." }
  { query: "callers", symbol: "foo", file: "src/foo.ts", depth: 2 }
  { query: "unused-exports", root: "." }
  { query: "dependencies", file: "src/bar.ts" }
  { query: "test-coverage", symbol: "add", file: "src/math.ts" }
  { query: "build-coverage", root: "." }

kind.description: Symbol kind filter (for symbol query): function, class, interface, type, variable, etc.

tools.name: smart_code_type_infer
tools.description:
Infer type information at a specific code location. Use when: need to know the exact type of a variable/expression, understand complex generic types, or verify type contracts across files.

Uses LSP hover capability. Requires a running TypeScript LSP server.

tools.name: smart_codebase_index
tools.description: Persistent codebase symbol index — build, update, query symbols, or generate a repo map.

command.description: Command: build (full scan), update (incremental), query (search symbols), map (repo map), stats (index statistics)

strategy.description: Update strategy: "hash" reads all files (default), "git" uses git diff --name-only (5-50x faster for large projects). Only used with command:"update"

root.description: Project root directory (default: current working directory)

symbol.description: Symbol name to search for (for query command)

kind.description: Filter by symbol kind (for query command)

include.description: Glob pattern for files to include (default: **/*.{js,mjs,cjs,ts,tsx,jsx,py,pyi,rs})

exclude.description: Glob pattern for files to exclude (default: **/node_modules/**)

tools.name: smart_compose
tools.description: Use when: need to compose multiple tool calls into a pipeline — sequential (pipe), parallel (fan-out), or conditional branching. Accepts a JSON pipeline definition and executes all steps. Avoid when: running a single tool (use the direct tool instead), or executing a workflow (use smart_workflow instead which has state persistence).

pipeline.description: JSON array of pipeline steps. Each step: { tool, args, mode: "seq"|"par"|"cond", condition?: { onField, match, then, else } }

tools.name: smart_coverage
tools.description: Use when: need to find untested code paths — uncovered branches, conditions, edge cases in if/else, switch, loops, ternaries. Call after writing new code to identify gaps. Avoid when: looking for test suggestions (use test_suggest instead).

tools.name: smart_cross_file_edit
tools.description: Use when: need to apply the same pattern change across multiple related files. Uses import graph to find all affected files. Dry-run by default. Avoid when: editing a single file (use standard edit tool instead).

qualityGates.message: Cross-file edit requires import dependency analysis first. Run ssr({tool:"import_graph"}).

tools.name: smart_db
tools.description:
Database query tools — read-only SQL access.
Operations: introspect (schema), query (SELECT only).
Supports SQLite (built-in) and PostgreSQL (optional pg npm).
Safety: read-only, timeout 10s, row limit 1000, DDL/DML blocked.
Use when: need to explore database schema, verify data, or answer data questions.

operation.description: introspect = show schema, query = run SELECT

connection.description: SQLite: path to .db file. PostgreSQL: postgresql: //<user>:<password>@<host>/<db>

sql.description: SQL query (SELECT only, required for query operation)

tools.name: smart_debug
tools.description: Use when: need to understand an error or stack trace — classifies error type, identifies root cause, suggests fixes. Paste the full error message. Avoid when: error is straightforward and known (use basic reasoning instead).

tools.name: smart_diagram
tools.description: Use when: need to visualize architecture, workflow, or data flow as Mermaid.js diagram. Supports flowchart, sequence, class, ER. Can convert import-graph output to visual. Useful for documentation or design discussions.

tools.name: smart_diff_view
tools.description: Use when: need to visually compare files or preview changes before applying. Generates terminal-friendly unified or side-by-side diffs. Solves the "no visual diff" weakness — use before fast_apply to verify changes.

file2.description: Second file path (optional — compare file1 vs file2)

content.description: New content to compare against file1 (for previewing proposed changes)

git.description: Show git diff (working tree vs HEAD) for file1

staged.description: Show staged git diff (with --git, shows staged changes)

color.description: Use ANSI color codes (default: true for terminal)

tools.name: smart_docx_generate
tools.description:
Generate APA 7th formatted Microsoft Word (.docx) documents programmatically.

Creates professional academic documents with:
  - Title page with centered title
  - Abstract section
  - Numbered body sections with proper heading hierarchy
  - Data tables with header rows
  - APA 7th reference list with hanging indents (0.5 inch)
  - Times New Roman 12pt, double-spaced

Input structure:
  { title, abstract?, sections: [{heading, content}], references: [...], tables: [{title, data}], outputPath? }

Examples:
  { title: "My Research", abstract: "This paper...", sections: [{heading:"Introduction", content:"..."}], references: ["Smith, J. (2024)..."], outputPath: "/tmp/paper.docx" }

sections.description: Body sections. Each: { heading: string, content: string|string[], level?: number }. Or plain strings for simple paragraphs.

content.description: String (split by double newlines) or array of paragraph strings

tables.description: Tables. Each: { title?: string, data: string[][], columnWidths?: number[] }

outputPath.description: Output file path (default: ./<title>.docx in current directory)

tools.name: smart_error_diagnose
tools.description: Use when: getting an error and need root cause analysis. Cross-references against known error KB + memory store (auto-searched). Covers build/runtime/test/permission/path/network. Supports auto-store successful diagnoses for future reuse.

noMemory.description: Skip memory store search (memory search is ON by default)

memoryResolution.description: Resolution text for store (defaults to KB fix)

tools.name: smart_exec
tools.description:
Execute code in a sandboxed environment. Supports bash, node, python, deno.
Safety: deno --allow-none by default (no fs, no net). Other languages run with limited permissions.
Use when: need to verify code works, run tests, or execute small scripts safely.
Avoid when: running production services, long-running processes, or untrusted third-party code.

mode.description: Execution mode: "run" (default) to execute, "verify" to validate code output (Phase 20)

permission.description: Permission level (default: none). write/net require confirmation.

testCases.description: Test cases for verification (verify mode only)

maxRetries.description: Max retries on failure for verify mode (default: 1)

tools.name: smart_git_commit
tools.description: Use when: you need to commit changes with an auto-generated conventional commit message. Analyzes staged diff, generates type (feat/fix/refactor/etc.) and scope, then optionally executes the commit. Supports --dry-run preview, --amend, --all auto-stage, and custom --type/--scope overrides.

message.description: Use this message instead of auto-generating (e.g., "feat: add login")

type.description: Force conventional commit type (default: auto-detect from diff)

scope.description: Add scope to commit message (e.g., "auth"). Empty string to force no scope.

all.description: Auto-stage all tracked files before committing

amend.description: Amend the last commit instead of creating a new one

dryRun.description: Preview the commit without actually committing

tools.name: smart_git_context
tools.description: Use when: need to understand current git state — staged/unstaged changes, commit diffs, and their impact scope via import graph. Run before committing or generating PR descriptions.

tools.name: smart_git_pr
tools.description: Use when: you need to create a Pull Request with an auto-generated description. Analyzes commits between head and base branches, generates a structured PR title and body, then optionally creates the PR via gh CLI. Supports --draft, --no-publish preview, and manual --title/--body overrides.

body.description: PR body text (default: auto-generated with commit list + file list)

noPublish.description: Don't create the PR, just show the preview

tools.name: smart_git_review
tools.description: Use when: you need a code review of working tree changes, a specific commit, or a remote PR. Analyzes diffs with heuristic checks (security, performance, correctness, style) and produces structured per-file comments with severity levels. Can also fetch and review PRs from GitHub.

commit.description: Review a specific commit (e.g., HEAD, HEAD~1)

pr.description: Review an existing PR by URL (e.g., https://github.com/user/repo/pull/123)

output.description: Write review report to file path

tools.name: smart_hallucination_check
tools.description:
Check LLM output for hallucinations (fabrication, misattribution, unfaithful, self-contradiction, off-topic, confident-refusal).

Runs 5 structural checks:
  1. Factual — are mentioned identifiers present in context?
  2. Consistency — are there internal contradictions?
  3. Groundedness — can conclusions be traced to context?
  4. Off-topic — does output address the query?
  5. Confidence — overconfident language without evidence?

DOI Verification mode (mode:"doi"):
  Extracts all DOIs from the output and verifies each via doi.org liveness check.
  Dead DOIs (404) = likely fabricated. Restricted (403) = paywalled but may exist.

Returns: { overallScore: 1-10, verdict: "pass"|"warn"|"fail", issues[], summary }

Examples:
  { output: "The bug is in parser.js...", context: "Error at parser.js:42" }
  { output: "...", query: "Why does the parser crash?", strictness: 7 }
  { output: "Smith (2024, doi:10.1234/fake) found...", mode: "doi" }

output.description: LLM output text to check for hallucinations (required)

context.description: Original tool output or context to verify against

query.description: Original user question for off-topic detection

toolName.description: Tool that produced the output (for context)

mode.description: Check mode: "default" (5 structural checks) or "doi" (DOI liveness verification). Default: "default".

tools.name: smart_hybrid_router
tools.description:
Universal Task Router — single entry point for ALL tasks. Just describe what you need.

Phase 3: LLM only describes the task. Router handles classification + routing.
  code tasks (structure/debug/search/change-impact/semantic)
    → deterministic CKG/LSP/grep execution
  general tasks (crawl/refactor/git/security/test/report/lang/search/edit/plan/office/wiki/analyze)
    → structured tool/skill recommendation + workflow

Features:
- Universal Classifier: categorizes into 6 code types + 13 general domains
- Code tasks: automatic CKG/LSP/grep execution with confidence scoring
- General tasks: returns best-matching skill/tools/workflow recommendation
- Hybrid fallback: uncertain questions gather context from both paths

Examples:
  { question: "who calls authenticate() in src/auth.ts", root: "." }
    → code structure: CKG callers query + LSP type info

  { question: "幫我爬 iyf.tv 的 API", root: "." }
    → general crawl: recommends skill("smart-mcp-crawl") + workflow

  { question: "explain the architecture of this module", files: ["src/lib/hybrid-engine.mjs"] }
    → semantic: AST + deps + file content for LLM synthesis

  { question: "掃描這個專案的漏洞", root: "." }
    → general security: recommends skill("smart-mcp-security") + tools

files.description: Relevant file paths to focus analysis

symbols.description: Symbol names to focus analysis (auto-extracted from question if not provided)

tools.name: smart_impact_flow
tools.description:
Full change-impact analysis pipeline. Use when: planning a refactor, reviewing a PR diff, need to know what depends on your changes, or want to predict which tests might break. Builds on CKG + LSP call graph + test file heuristics.

Three input modes:
  1. diff (git diff text) → auto-detect changed symbols → CKG propagation → test prediction
  2. files + symbols → skip diff parsing, directly trace symbols through call graph
  3. files only → auto-detect symbols in files → propagate → test prediction

Output: structured JSON with direct/transitive impacts, affected test files, confidence score, human-readable summary.

diff.description: git diff text to analyze (alternative to files+symbols)

files.description: Array of file paths to analyze (alternative to diff)

symbols.description: Specific symbol names to trace (used with files)

depth.description: Impact propagation depth (1-3, default: 2). depth=1=direct only, depth=2=direct+transitive

predictTests.description: Whether to predict affected test files (default: true)

tools.name: smart_import_graph
tools.description: Use when: need to understand cross-file import dependencies before refactoring. Supports JS/TS, Python, Ruby, Rust, Go. Configure depth for shallow or deep analysis. Use focus to zoom in on specific files. Auto-uses codebase index if available (5-50x faster).

tools.name: smart_ingest_document
tools.description:
Convert binary documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, HTML, RTF, PPTX, etc.) to Markdown text that the LLM can read and analyze.

Supports:
  - PDF: full text extraction + auto-OCR fallback for scanned PDFs
  - DOCX: converts to Markdown preserving headings, lists, emphasis
  - HTML: strips markup, preserves links and structure
  - XLSX/XLS: converts sheet data to Markdown tables
  - RTF: macOS textutil-based conversion
  - PPTX: slide text extraction (requires python-pptx or pptx2md)
  - Plain text: Markdown, CSV, JSON, YAML, XML — reads directly

OCR (for scanned/image-only PDFs):
  - Auto-detects scanned PDFs when text extraction yields no content
  - Uses pdftoppm + tesseract for OCR
  - Force OCR mode: ocr: true (skip text extraction)
  - Custom language: ocrLang: "chi_tra+eng" (tesseract language pack required)

Auto-registers in document registry for cross-session lookup.
Use smart_list_documents to search previously ingested documents.

For large PDFs (>100 pages), use offset/limit to read in chunks.
  Example: { path: "spec.pdf" }                  → first 100 pages
           { path: "spec.pdf", limit: 50 }       → first 50 pages
           { path: "spec.pdf", offset: 50, limit: 50 } → pages 51-100
           { path: "scan.pdf", ocr: true }       → force OCR mode
           { path: "scan.pdf", ocr: true, ocrLang: "chi_tra+eng" } → OCR with Chinese

path.description: Absolute path to the document file

offset.description: Page offset for paginated formats (default: 0, PDF only)

limit.description: Max pages to return (default: all, PDF only)

summary.description: Optional short summary of the document content (saved in registry for later search)

ocr.description: Force OCR mode for PDF (skip text extraction, default: false)

tools.name: smart_integrate
tools.description: Use when: need meta-operations on smart itself — list available tools, suggest commit scope, generate PR descriptions, diagnose terminal errors, or manage MCP servers. Start here when unsure which tool to use (command:"list") or before committing (command:"suggest-commit").

tools.name: smart_kg
tools.description:
Knowledge Graph memory — structured entity-relation storage.
Operations: create_entities, create_relations, search_nodes, open_nodes, read_graph, delete_entities, delete_observations, delete_relations.
Use when: need to remember structured relationships (who works on what, which module depends on which, etc.).
Complements semantic memory (smart_memory_store) which is better for similarity search.

tools.name: smart_list_documents
tools.description:
Search and list previously ingested documents (cross-session persistent index).

Documents are auto-registered when read via smart_ingest_document.
The index persists across sessions at ~/.smart/cache/documents.db.

Use cases:
  - "What documents did I read last time?"
  - "Find that contract I read yesterday"
  - "Show me all PDFs I've analyzed"
  - "Search documents about protocol specs"

query.description: Optional search term — matches title, path, and summary (case-insensitive)

format.description: Filter by format (pdf, docx, xlsx, html, etc.)

tools.name: smart_mcts_plan
tools.description:
MCTS-based tool planning — uses Monte Carlo Tree Search to find the optimal tool chain for complex multi-step tasks.
Use when: task requires 5+ steps, involves multiple files/tools, or static routing is uncertain.
Avoid when: simple tasks with clear tool mappings (use smart_agent_recommend instead).
Available tools to consider (default: all standard tools)

useFallback.description: Fall back to static matching if MCTS times out (default: true)

tools.name: smart_memory_store
tools.description: Use when: need to store or retrieve past error resolutions, patterns, and learnings across sessions. Supports fuzzy matching + vector search (TF-IDF hybrid) — use vector=true for semantic matching across similar error messages. Use search first, add if not found. Also supports type:"skill_patch" for storing reusable behavior patterns (trigger_conditions + behavior_change + target_skill).

command.description: store/search/list/get/confirm/delete/stats/export/extract/quality — extract auto-generates skill_patches from findings (pipe JSON via stdin); quality shows memory health dashboard (requires --db)

query.description: Error message or trigger description to search or store (positional for store/search)

type.description: Entry type: "error" (default) for error resolutions, "skill_patch" for reusable behavior patterns

targetSkill.description: Target skill name this skill_patch applies to (for type:skill_patch)

behaviorChange.description: What to do differently — the behavior improvement (for type:skill_patch)

resolution.description: How the error was fixed (for store)

tools.description: Comma-separated tool names used (for store)

limit.description: Max results (default: 10 for search, 50 for list)

vector.description: Enable hybrid vector search (TF-IDF + fuzzy) for better semantic matching

findingsFile.description: Path to findings JSON file (for extract command)

minFrequency.description: Minimum occurrences to trigger skill_patch (default: 2, for extract command)

ttl.description: Auto-expire after duration (e.g. "7d", "30d", "1h") — for store command

keep.description: Prevent auto-cleanup for this entry — for store command

includeArchived.description: Include archived entries in search/list results

agent.description: Agent ID for cross-agent memory (e.g. "claude-code", "opencode", "codex"). Auto-detected from env if not specified. Use "all" to search across all agents.

tools.name: smart_model_router
tools.description:
Multi-Model Orchestration — routes tasks to optimal cost/tier.

Phase 14: Dynamic tier selection for token efficiency and cost optimization.
Classifies tasks into 4 tiers and routes accordingly:

  Tier 1 (deterministic, $0)    — CKG, LSP, grep: "who calls foo()"
  Tier 2 (local small, $0.001)  — Local model: "what does this do?"
  Tier 3 (medium API, $0.01)    — Medium model: "debug this error"
  Tier 4 (strong API, $0.05)    — Strongest model: "refactor this module"

Commands:
  route   → classify a task and estimate cost
  report  → show cost tracking report
  suggest → suggest optimal tier + tool from a natural language question
  savings → estimate savings vs all-T4 baseline
  tool    → suggest tier for a specific tool name
  tiers   → list all defined tiers
  reset   → reset cost tracking counters

command.description: Command: route (classify), report (cost), suggest (question→tier), savings (estimate), tool (tier for tool), tiers (list), reset (counters)

tier.description: Target tier: "auto" (default), "T1"-"T4", or tier ID 1-4 (for "route" command)

tool.description: Tool name to suggest tier for (for "tool" command, e.g. "smart_grep")

callPattern.description: Call pattern for savings estimation (for "savings" command, e.g. {"t1": 100, "t2": 50, "t3": 20, "t4": 5})

tools.name: smart_naming
tools.description: Use when: need to audit naming conventions across the codebase (kebab-case, camelCase, PascalCase, UPPER_CASE). Run to detect violations before code review or to enforce team naming standards.

tools.name: smart_obsidian_write
tools.description:
Write a note directly to an Obsidian vault with YAML frontmatter.

Auto-detects vault location from:
  1. OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH environment variable
  2. Common macOS locations (~/Documents/Obsidian Vault, etc.)

Features:
  - YAML frontmatter with title, date, tags, category
  - Auto-placement in vault subfolders
  - Sanitized filenames from titles
  - Overwrite protection

Examples:
  { title: "My Research Note", content: "Findings...", tags: ["research", "ai"] }
  { title: "Meeting Notes", content: "...", folder: "meetings", vault: "/path/to/vault" }
  { title: "Update", content: "...", overwrite: true }

vault.description: Obsidian vault path (auto-detected if not provided)

folder.description: Subfolder within vault (created if not exists)

tools.name: smart_patch_gen
tools.description:
Generate edit patches from analysis tool output.

Bridges the gap between analysis (error_diagnose/debug/thinking) and action (cross_file_edit).

Input: analysis output text from any smart-mcp tool.
Output: structured patch plan with file paths, line numbers, and change descriptions.

Safety:
  - preview=true (default): shows changes without applying
  - 3+ files require explicit apply=true to proceed

Integration:
  error_diagnose → patch_gen(preview) → review → cross_file_edit(apply)

Examples:
  { source: "error_diagnose", content: "..." }
  { source: "debug", content: "Fix: rename foo to bar in src/app.js", file: "src/app.js" }
  { source: "thinking", content: "...", preview: false, apply: true }

Phase 8: Completes the "analyze → fix" loop.

content.description: Analysis output text to extract patches from (required)

source.description: Source tool hint — improves parsing accuracy (default: auto)

file.description: Optional explicit target file (when content lacks file paths)

pattern.description: Optional explicit target text pattern to replace

preview.description: Preview mode (default: true). Shows patch plan without applying.

apply.description: Actually apply changes via cross_file_edit (default: false, requires explicit opt-in for 3+ files)

tools.name: smart_planner
tools.description: Use when: need to decompose a goal into steps, track execution state, or re-plan on failure. Supports plan generation + execution state (execute/next/report/replan commands). Use its output as input for smart_deep_think plan_execute template. For full lifecycle: execute → next → report (repeats) → done. On step failure, auto-replans remaining steps.

command.description: plan (default, generate plan only) | execute (plan + state file) | next (get next step) | report (record result) | replan (force re-plan) | analyze (tool sequence) | list-tasks (show templates)

tools.description: Comma-separated tool names (for analyze)

strict.description: Only use tools that exist in registry

state.description: Path to plan state file (for next/report/replan)

stepStatus.description: Step execution status (for report, default: ok)

result.description: Step result as JSON string (for report)

error.description: Error message if step failed (for report)

duration.description: Step execution duration in ms (for report)

tools.name: smart_pr_review
tools.description:
Automated PR review — combines git diff, security scan, code impact, and LSP diagnostics.
Produces structured review: Security, Impact, Code Quality, Summary.
Use when: reviewing a PR, checking changes before merge, or analyzing branch differences.

tools.name: smart_progress
tools.description: Check progress of long-running tasks. View active tasks, their completion percentage, and recent messages.

command.description: Command: status (check one task), all (list all tasks), clear (remove completed tasks)

taskId.description: Task ID to check (for status command)

tools.name: smart_pw_browser
tools.description:
Control a web browser — navigate, click, fill forms, screenshot, execute JavaScript.

Commands:
  navigate(url):   Browse to a URL
  snapshot():      Get page text + links
  click(selector): Click element (text or CSS selector)
  fill(selector, value): Fill a form field
  screenshot():    Capture page as PNG (base64)
  run_code(code):  Execute JavaScript in the page

NOT for: reading article content (use smart_exa_crawl with --clean).
NOT for: searching the web (use smart_exa_search).

url.description: URL to navigate to (required for navigate)

selector.description: CSS selector or text content for click/fill

value.description: Value to fill in form field (required for fill)

code.description: JavaScript code to execute in page context (required for run_code)

fullPage.description: Capture full page screenshot (default: true, screenshot only)

tools.name: smart_py_helper
tools.description: Use when: need to analyze Python project health — detect venv status, check dependency consistency, run mypy type checking, get modernization recommendations. Run when setting up new Python project or before migration.

tools.name: smart_refactor_plan
tools.description:
CKG-based refactoring assistant.

Analyzes API usage patterns across the codebase and generates a structured,
ordered migration plan with safety gates.

Steps:
1. Queries CKG for all callers of the given API symbol
2. Classifies each usage into patterns (direct-call, class-method, event-handler, module-init, factory)
3. Generates an ordered migration plan grouped by file and risk level
4. Applies safety gates for high-impact migrations

Output includes:
- Usage pattern breakdown with counts
- File-by-file migration steps (ordered by dependency)
- Risk assessment per step (low/medium/high)
- Overall difficulty score (1-10)

Examples:
  { symbol: "oldApi", file: "src/utils.ts" }
  { symbol: "deprecatedFunc", file: "src/helpers.ts", newApi: "newFunc", goal: "Replace legacy helper" }
  { symbol: "OldComponent", file: "src/components/old.tsx", plan: false, format: "json" }

safetyThreshold.description: Warn if more than N files affected (default: 5)

excludeFiles.description: Files to exclude from migration plan

tools.name: smart_rename_safety
tools.description: Use when: need to rename a function/variable/file across the codebase and want to detect naming conflicts, shadowing, or incomplete renames before applying. Dry-run by default — safe to use without side effects.

tools.name: smart_report
tools.description: Use when: need to generate a self-contained HTML report from test results, security scan data, or coverage analysis. Best for sharing results with team or archiving. Call after running smart_test, smart_security, or coverage.

tools.name: smart_edit
tools.description: Use when: need to edit a file with exact string replacement. Supports dry-run (default), exact matching, regex mode, and multi-file editing. Returns nextCommand for LLM to directly apply changes.

oldString.description: Text to find and replace (exact match by default, regex if regex=true)

files.description: Multiple files to edit (alternative to file)

regex.description: Use regex matching instead of exact (default: false)

tools.name: smart_research
tools.description:
Research a URL end-to-end. No need to worry about crawl parameters — just pick depth.

  depth=quick:      Fetch + clean + markdown (~2s, most pages)
  depth=deep:       Same + quality analysis with tips (~3s)
  depth=exhaustive: Same + chunk by heading (~5s, long articles)

Do NOT use for: searching (use smart_exa_search). Crawling without processing (use smart_exa_crawl).

depth.description: Research depth. quick: basic fetch (~2s). deep: fetch + quality analysis (~3s). exhaustive: fetch + chunk for long articles (~5s).

tools.name: smart_rs_helper
tools.description: Use when: need to analyze Rust project health — run cargo check for compilation errors, cargo clippy for lint warnings, analyze Cargo.toml dependencies, check formatting. Run after modifying Rust code or before committing.

command.description: check (cargo check), clippy (cargo clippy), analyze (project analysis), fmt (format check)

tools.name: smart_schedule
tools.description: Schedule recurring background tasks with cron expressions. Tasks run automatically and results are stored in memory.

command.description: Command: add (schedule), list (show all), remove (delete), status (check), run-now (execute immediately)

schedule.description: Cron expression: "min hour dom month dow" (e.g., "0 9 * * *" for daily at 9am)

task.description: Task description — what to do when triggered (e.g., "Run security scan")

notify.description: Store result in memory for next session (default: true)

tools.name: smart_search_docs
tools.description:
Full-text search within previously ingested document content.

Searches the content, title, and summary of documents you've previously
read with smart_ingest_document. Multiple search terms are combined with AND.

Examples:
  { query: "timing constraints" }          → find docs mentioning both terms
  { query: "Q&A bridge mode", limit: 5 }  → top 5 results
  { query: "contract termination" }        → find contract clauses

Use smart_list_documents to browse by metadata instead.

query.description: Search terms (space-separated = AND, searches content + title + summary)

tools.name: smart_test_suggest
tools.description: Use when: need to find missing test coverage for a file or git diff. Analyzes code to suggest edge cases, error paths, and main flows. Useful before committing new or modified code.

tools.name: smart_tool_stats
tools.description: Use when: need to review smart usage patterns — which tools are used most, error rates, trends. Also provides recommendations for stale tools. Run "patterns" to discover best tool combos and task-type effectiveness. Run periodically to identify overused or underused tools.

tools.name: smart_ts_helper
tools.description: Use when: need to analyze TypeScript project health — tsconfig strictness recommendations, detect unused exports, check ESM/CJS compatibility. Run after adding new TS files or before switching to strict mode.

tools.name: smart_workflow
tools.description: Use when: need to run a multi-tool workflow (debug/refactor/security/research), track step execution, report results, dispatch tool execution, or re-plan after failure. Supports composite templates that orchestrate multiple tools in dependency order. Commands: create (from template) | report (step result) | dispatch (execute step/group) | replan (after failure) | summary (state) | list-templates.

command.description: create (start new workflow from template) | report (record step result) | dispatch (execute steps by spawning CLI tools) | replan (re-plan after failure) | summary (show workflow state) | list-templates (show available templates)

template.description: Workflow template name: debug-flow, refactor-flow, refactor-safe-flow, security-flow, research-flow, default-flow, git-flow (for create)

state.description: Path to workflow state file (for dispatch/report/replan/summary)

step.description: Step number to dispatch or report (for dispatch/report)

stepStatus.description: Step execution status (for report, default: ok)

result.description: Step result as JSON string (for report)

error.description: Error message if step failed (for report)

duration.description: Step execution duration in ms (for report)

timeout.description: Per-tool timeout in ms (for dispatch, default: 30000)

context.description: Additional context for plan generation (for create/replan)
